problem.statement: Client, partner, captain and field actors need one authenticated read-only view of their WLT-owned wallet, balances and ledger references through DSH BFF, while finance operators need permission-scoped lookup without transferring financial ownership to DSH or any frontend.
problem.currentWorkaround: Field finance has a partial wallet flow, captain finance exposes COD only, partner finance presents settlement data as a wallet, client has no wallet view, and canonical frontend routes do not all match the registered DSH router.

actors.role: Authenticated customer reading only their own wallet and ledger references
permittedActions: read own available pending and held balances

actors.role: Authenticated partner reading only their own wallet and reference finance
permittedActions: read own settlements commissions and payouts

actors.role: Authenticated captain reading their wallet, earnings references, payouts and COD liability
Authenticated field actor reading their wallet, commissions, ledger and payout requests
permittedActions: read and submit own payout requests

actors.role: Permission-scoped finance operator reading representative wallets and ledger references
permittedActions: inspect reference commissions settlements and payouts
forbiddenActions: use read permission for a money-moving action
treat DSH as financial truth owner

routesOrScreens: field finance wallet commissions ledger and payouts

outcome.statement: Every representative sees an authenticated WLT-owned wallet and permission-scoped ledger view through DSH, while operators can inspect supported wallets and matching ledgers without any DSH or frontend balance mutation path.
outcome.primaryMetric: actor-scoped wallet and ledger read coverage across required surfaces
guardrailMetrics: frontend direct WLT financial read count
DSH wallet balance mutation path count
outcome.baselineState: Wallet capability is fragmented and route bindings are inconsistent.
outcome.targetState: All four actor surfaces use canonical identity-scoped DSH reads and the control panel uses finance.read-scoped DSH lookups backed by WLT truth.
outcome.observationWindow: targeted contract, backend, frontend, runtime, finance-control and independent acceptance verification

criteria: WLT accepts only client partner captain and field wallet actor types.
DSH derives self-service actor identity from the authenticated session and never from a frontend query or body.
Client partner captain and field have canonical own-wallet and own-ledger routes.
The control panel wallet and ledger lookup requires finance.read and validates actor type and id.
Available pending held earned settled and paid balances are rendered from WLT without local derivation.
Ledger entries are actor-scoped before forwarding to WLT.
Partner wallet UI does not use settlement pending amount as available wallet balance and contains no hardcoded actor fallback.
Field payout requests remain idempotent and cannot exceed the loaded available balance in the UI.
No DSH or frontend route writes wallet balances or appends ledger truth for this journey.
Loading empty partial forbidden offline error and retry states are represented on required human-facing surfaces.
Correlation and no-store response controls are applied to representative finance reads.
requiredChecks: DSH actor route and scoping tests

business: WLT is the sole owner of wallet and ledger truth.
DSH is an authenticated and authorized BFF only.
Every self-service wallet read is scoped to the resolved actor.
A wallet is unique by actor type and actor id.
Ledger history is append-only financial evidence.
negative: No DSH table or handler mutates representative wallet balances.
No frontend calls internal WLT financial routes directly.
No user-facing surface supplies an arbitrary self-service actor id.
No settlement summary is labeled as a wallet balance.
No read permission authorizes a money-moving action.

owners.productOwner: WLT Wallet and Ledger Product Owner

execution.appetite: Close JRN-033 actor-scoped wallet, ledger, route, UI and boundary gaps without enabling unapproved money movement or production deployment.
fixedConstraints: Repository mode is REMOTE_ONLY on sambassam.
No PR merge force push release or production deployment is authorized.
Independent financial and product approvals cannot be self-issued by engineering.
